relationships.description: Astra Cloud connection: gRPC URI and application token used together in Token auth mode
Self-hosted Stargate connection: gRPC endpoint, table-based auth endpoint, username, password, and TLS toggle used together in Credentials auth mode
Basic-auth username/password pair posted to the Stargate table-based auth endpoint

instructions.msg: Two authentication modes are available, selected via jsonData.authKind: 0 = 'Token' (default; DataStax Astra Cloud) and 1 = 'Credentials' (self-hosted Stargate). authKind is a JSON number, not a string. Only the fields for the selected mode are read by the backend; the other mode's values are ignored but not cleared when switching.

To configure Token authentication (Astra Cloud), set jsonData.authKind to 0, put the Astra gRPC URI in jsonData.uri (e.g. $ASTRA_CLUSTER_ID-$ASTRA_REGION.apps.astra.datastax.com:443), and put the Astra application token in secureJsonData.token. Minimal payload: {"jsonData":{"authKind":0,"uri":"<cluster-id>-<region>.apps.astra.datastax.com:443"},"secureJsonData":{"token":"AstraCS:<token>"}}. Both values are mandatory: CheckHealth returns 'Invalid AstraDB URL' or 'Invalid AstraDB Token' if either is empty.

To configure Credentials authentication (self-hosted Stargate), set jsonData.authKind to 1, provide jsonData.grpcEndpoint (Stargate gRPC listener, host:port), jsonData.authEndpoint (Stargate REST auth listener, host:port), jsonData.user, jsonData.secure (boolean; true = TLS/https, false = plaintext/http), and put the password in secureJsonData.password. Minimal payload: {"jsonData":{"authKind":1,"grpcEndpoint":"stargate:8090","authEndpoint":"stargate:8081","user":"cassandra","secure":true},"secureJsonData":{"password":"<password>"}}. When secure=true the backend calls https://<authEndpoint>/v1/auth; when false it calls http://<authEndpoint>/v1/auth. All four jsonData fields plus the password are required — CheckHealth fails with a specific 'GRPC Endpoint Required' / 'Auth Endpoint Required' / 'User Name Required' / 'Password Required' message when any is empty.

Legacy interpretation: authKind is a JSON number (0 or 1) — not a string. Old datasources that stored no authKind at all effectively read as 0 (Token) because the frontend uses `jsonData.authKind || Connection.TOKEN` (which resolves to 0), and the backend struct AuthType (uint8) zero-values to AuthTypeToken. Do not assume a missing authKind means the datasource is unconfigured; treat it as Token mode and validate uri + token.

secureJsonData values (token, password) are write-only: they can be set or reset but never read back. To check whether a secret is already configured on an existing datasource, read the secureJsonFields object (e.g. secureJsonFields.token == true) instead of secureJsonData. Note the editor sets secureJsonFields.token=false while the SecretFormField is being edited — a temporary state that clears on save.

Connection endpoints are raw host:port strings, not URLs — do not prefix them with http:// or https://. jsonData.uri is passed directly to grpc.NewClient with TLS enabled. jsonData.grpcEndpoint is passed to grpc.NewClient (TLS only when jsonData.secure=true). jsonData.authEndpoint has scheme prepended by the backend as fmt.Sprintf('https://%s/v1/auth', authEndpoint) or 'http://...' depending on jsonData.secure. A stray scheme prefix produces an invalid URL like https://https://host/v1/auth.
